feed.title: SAP Community - SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central
feed.subtitle: SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central blog posts in SAP Community

entry.title: Conditional Authentications for Various Employee Class(SAP SuccessFactors) : User Types (IAS)
entry.content:
REQUIREMENT:

The business is setting up Single Sign-On (SSO) for internal employees, but external vendors must authenticate using passwords. Internal employees will log in via Microsoft SSO configured through IAS, while external vendors will log in using credentials via IAS.

To set up conditional authentication in IAS, employees must be assigned the correct user type to apply the appropriate authentication method.

To achieve this, a custom solution was set up. The Employee Class(custom03) field from SAP SuccessFactors will map to one of the custom attributes in the IAS system. Based on the value of this custom attribute, the user type field should be updated accordingly, hence updating the authentication method.

SOLUTION & IMPLEMENTATION STEPS:

STEP 1 - Add Source and Target code to update the 'Employee Class(Custom03)' field from SAP SuccessFactors to 'Custom Attribute 5' in the SAP Identity Provisioning Service (IAS).

  • Source Code - Navigate to Identity Provisioning -> Source System -> Click on SAP SuccessFactors system -> Transformation-> Click on Edit Code -> Update the below code -> Save
  {
"sourcePath": "$.custom03",
"targetPath": "$['urn:sap:cloud:scim:schemas:extension:sfsf:2.0:User']['custom03']"
}, 

  • Target Code - Navigate to Identity Provisioning -> Target System -> Click on SAP Identity Authentication system -> Transformation-> Click on Edit Code -> Update the below code -> Save
{
"sourcePath": "$['urn:sap:cloud:scim:schemas:extension:sfsf:2.0:User']['custom03']",
"optional": true,
"targetPath": "$['urn:sap:cloud:scim:schemas:extension:custom:2.0:User']['attributes'][4]['value']"
},
{
"condition": "$['urn:sap:cloud:scim:schemas:extension:sfsf:2.0:User']['custom03'] EMPTY false",
"constant": "customAttribute5",
"targetPath": "$['urn:sap:cloud:scim:schemas:extension:custom:2.0:User']['attributes'][4]['name']"
},
  • Run the Read Job to update the data

 Navigate to Identity Provisioning -> Source System -> Click on SAP SuccessFactors system -> Jobs-> Click on 'Run Now'.

The data will be updated for all the employees in IAS with the Custom Attribute 5 field flowing from Employee Class from SuccessFactors.

 

STEP 2 - Setting Conditional Logic for User Types

Once the 'Employee Class' is successfully mapped to 'Custom Attribute 5' in IAS, the next step is to define conditional logic to update the userType field based on the value of 'Custom Attribute 5'. This classification helps in categorizing users into different types.

Add Source and Target code with different conditions to update the User Type field in IAS. Conditions are as below:

  • If Employee Class/Custom Attribute 5 = Contractor, User Type = External
  • If Employee Class/Custom Attribute 5 = Employee, User Type = Employee
  • If Employee Class/Custom Attribute 5 = Support, User Type = Partner
  • If Employee Class/Custom Attribute 5 = API, User Type = Public

These conditions could vary depending on the business needs.

  • Source Code - Navigate to Identity Provisioning -> Source System -> Click on SAP SuccessFactors system -> Transformation-> Click on Edit Code -> Update the below code -> Save

Custom3 denotes employee class in SuccessFactors, this key can be varied depending upon your system configuration.

{
"condition": "($.custom03 == 'Contractor')",
"constant": "External",
"targetPath": "$.userType"
},
{
"condition": "($.custom03 == 'Employee')",
"constant": "Employee",
"targetPath": "$.userType"
},
{
"condition": "($.custom03 == 'Support')",
"constant": "Partner",
"targetPath": "$.userType"
},
{
"condition": "($.custom03 == 'API')",
"constant": "Public",
"targetPath": "$.userType"
},

 

  • Run the Read Job to update the data

Navigate to Identity Provisioning -> Source System -> Click on SAP SuccessFactors system -> Jobs-> Click on 'Run Now'.

 

Once the job is completed, the UserType field will be updated based on the conditions.

 

 

STEP 3 - Setup Conditional Authentication

The final step in the process is configuring conditional authentication rules in IAS. These rules determine the authentication method based on the user type.

Navigate to Applications & Resources -> Applications ->SuccessFactors System -> Conditional Authentication.

Click on the Add button and you can select criteria, one of the criteria is User- Type field. Select the User Type criteria to authenticate via a specific system. In this case, we want Employees to authenticate via Microsoft, Public with the IAS, and Partner with another service provider.

 

This can be customized as per the requirements but service providers that will be used as authentication system needs to be implemented before setting up conditional authentication.

For more detailed guidance on implementing Single Sign-On and setting up authentication via Microsoft, refer to the blog

Once the conditional authentications are saved, you can try logging with different user Types, authentication methods/system will be updated.

CONCLUSION

By following these steps, the business can achieve a secure and efficient authentication system customized to the needs of both internal employees and external vendors. This setup not only enhances security by leveraging SSO for internal employees but also provides flexibility to handle various user types appropriately.

This approach ensures a seamless and secure authentication experience, promoting productivity and security within the organization.

Thank you for taking the time to read this blog. Your feedback, questions, and suggestions are highly valued. Happy to hear your experiences, custom requirements, or any insights in the comments section below.

Happy learning 🙂

entry.title: How to search for transactions in English even if your language is a different one
entry.content:
With the last 2024H2 release, the enhancement requested by @Dirk_Witkowski has become true.

Now it's possible to work in different languages but continue using English transactions. 

The procedure is to enable the permission in the "permission Roles" transaction as shown below.

 

After enabling this permission, the admin users/consultants can use English for navigation. On the other hand, common users still can use only their native language. This is helpful because an additional language leads to more search results and can overwhelm the common users. Normally, for them it should be disabled.

 

Not everything is perfect

Please note that the "search action bar" won't show the transaction in your language. Instead it will show the translated transaction.

 

 

Hope this blog helps!

Give me a Kudo!

entry.title: Reshaping the Time Tracking Experience with SAP SuccessFactors
entry.content:
Introduction

The Clock In Clock Out feature within SAP SuccessFactors Time Tracking offers an intuitive and efficient solution for accurately capturing employee work hours. Seamlessly integrated with the broader SuccessFactors Timesheet processes, this feature allows organizations to record employee in and out times through various interfaces, including third-party clocking systems and SAP SuccessFactors web and mobile platforms. By ensuring accurate time tracking, it supports compliance with labor laws, streamlines payroll processing, and enhances overall workforce.

Below is a quick overview of the feature.

Tech Requirements

Clock In Clock Out Set Up

The Time Tracking feature involves two set of configurations:

  1. Creation of Time Events, Time Groups and Pairing Events - SAP SuccessFactors allows for the creation of different Time Events, which can be selected during the clock in or clock out process. Time Groups combine these events into a set that can be assigned to specific employees, granting them access to various time type actions. The Pairing Engine completes the pairing cycle, ensuring that each event is accurately recorded and processed.
  2. Time Information Fields which resides on Job Information - These fields are essential for time tracking because they store critical data about an employee's work schedule, time recording method, and time management settings. These fields define the employee's working hours, time profile, and other relevant parameters, which are necessary for accurately tracking and evaluating their time-related activities.

 

Key Features of SAP SuccessFactors Time Tracking

SAP SuccessFactors Time Tracking is more than just a tool for clocking in and out; it’s a versatile solution designed to meet the diverse needs of modern organizations. Here’s a closer look at some of its key features:

  • Clock In Clock Out Functionality - The Clock In Clock Out feature is at the heart of SAP SuccessFactors Time Tracking. This intuitive application allows employees to record their start and end times through various interfaces, including third-party clocking systems, SAP SuccessFactors’ web interface, and mobile quick punch actions. Seamlessly integrated with the existing Timesheet processes, this functionality ensures that all recorded times are captured accurately and available for payroll processing.
  • Comprehensive Reporting and Analytics - Time Tracking in SAP SuccessFactors also includes powerful reporting and analytics capabilities. Managers can generate detailed reports on employee work hours, overtime, and attendance, helping them make informed decisions about workforce management. These reports also assist in meeting compliance requirements by providing accurate documentation of employee work hours.
  • Geolocation Tracking - For organizations with a mobile or remote workforce, SAP SuccessFactors Time Tracking offers geolocation tracking capabilities. This feature allows the system to capture the location of an employee when they clock in or out, ensuring that the recorded time accurately reflects where the work was performed. This is particularly useful for companies with field employees or those operating across multiple sites.
  • Real-Time Absence Management - SAP SuccessFactors Time Tracking integrates seamlessly with absence management, allowing employees to request time off directly through the system. Managers can view and approve requests in real-time, ensuring that all absences are tracked and accounted for. This integration helps prevent scheduling conflicts and ensures that time off is appropriately documented and factored into payroll and resource planning.
  • Automatic Overtime Calculation - The system can automatically calculate overtime based on predefined rules, such as exceeding standard working hours or working on weekends or holidays. This feature eliminates the need for manual calculations, reducing errors and ensuring that employees are compensated accurately for their extra hours.
  • Integration with Payroll Systems - SAP SuccessFactors Time Tracking integrates seamlessly with various payroll systems, ensuring that all tracked hours, including regular time, overtime, and absences, are accurately reflected in payroll calculations. This integration reduces the risk of errors and ensures that employees are paid correctly and on time.
  • User-Friendly Interface - The time tracking feature in SAP SuccessFactors is designed with a user-friendly interface, making it easy for employees and managers to navigate. The mobile app version is particularly beneficial for employees on the go, allowing them to quickly clock in or out, request time off, or view their work schedules from anywhere.

 

Access and Enable the Feature

Licensing and Provisioning:

  • Before we can use Time Tracking, the organization must have the appropriate licenses. SAP SuccessFactors customers can work with their account representatives to ensure that Time Tracking is included in their subscription.
  • Once licensed, the Time Tracking feature must be provisioned within the SAP SuccessFactors system. This typically involves configuring the necessary permissions and settings within the system’s backend. You can refer Implementation guide for the steps.

Configuration in the Admin Center:

  • Administrators can access and configure the Time Tracking feature through the Admin Center in SAP SuccessFactors.
  • The setup process involves defining time-related settings, such as time types, work schedules, time profiles, and holiday calendars. These configurations determine how time is tracked and processed for different employee groups.
  • We can also set up time event types (e.g., clock in, clock out, break start, break end) and assign them to specific employees or groups.

Integration with Other Modules:

  • Time Tracking can be integrated with other SAP SuccessFactors modules, such as Payroll, Employee Central, and Absence Management. This integration allows for seamless data flow, ensuring that tracked hours are accurately reflected in payroll calculations, leave balances, and compliance reports.
  • We can also integrate with third-party clocking systems or hardware, such as biometric terminals, to capture time events directly.

UI of the Time Tracking Feature

Accessing the Web Application:

The SAP SuccessFactors homepage features a Clock Time tile that allows employees to clock in and out. These entries are automatically updated in the time sheet and included in reports.

To clock in, employees simply click the Clock In button on the tile, which records their start time. When it’s time to end their shift or take a break, they click the Clock Out button to record the end time. The system instantly updates these entries in the employee’s time sheet.

 

Accessing the Mobile App:

Employees can download and install the SAP SuccessFactors mobile app on their smartphones or tablets. After logging in, they can access the Clock Time feature directly from the app’s main screen.

The mobile app allows employees to clock in and out with a single tap, making it easy to record their work hours from anywhere, whether they’re in the office, working remotely, or on the field. The app may also support geolocation, enabling the system to capture the location of the clock in or out event, ensuring accuracy for employees who work in multiple locations.

Just like the web interface, the mobile app provides access to the employee’s time sheet, where they can review and submit their recorded hours. The mobile interface is designed to be user-friendly, with quick access to key features and a clear overview of recorded time entries.

Conclusion

The Clock In Clock Out feature in SAP SuccessFactors Time Tracking is a critical tool for modern workforce management, offering numerous benefits such as improved compliance, enhanced productivity, and reduced labor costs. By providing accurate tracking and reporting of employee work hours, this feature not only supports efficient payroll processing but also contributes to increased employee satisfaction and better resource allocation, ultimately driving organizational success.

Thank you for reading this blog. I hope this overview has provided you with a understanding of the time tracking feature. Your feedback, questions, and suggestions are always welcome. We’d love to hear about your experiences, custom requirements, or any insights you have, so please feel free to share them in the comments section below. Happy Learning 🙂

entry.title: Pernr can't be used as it is already assigned to a different Userid
entry.content:
Mistakes are quite common be it Development or Quality or Production. One such scenario is when the userid was entered wrong for an employee when he was onboarded on Employee Central Production. Due to the need of real-time integration, before the administrator could realise the mistake the user has been updated to SAP HCM system. 

Administrator was under the impression of the solution being straight forward, went ahead and made the change in SuccessFactors by correcting the user id and guess what ? SAP HCM triggers an error on SLG1 that Pernr can't be used as it is already assigned to a different Userid

Cause of the issue: 
When ever an employee is replicated from SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central to SAP HCM, Integration would create a key mapping table holding the values of PERNR, USER_ID and PERSON_ID. When the PERNER and USER_ID combination doesn't match and this value(inter changeably) is in use by a different id then we receive this error. 

How to fix: 

One might say we can use the report : RPUDELPN to delete the PERNR and re-replicate the user but then this can not be used in HCM if you HCM system is marked as PRODUCTION. 

One another way is to change the values on the table directly. Here are the steps for it. 

Step 1 : go to se16 and look for the table : ECPAO_EE_KMAP

Step 2 : enter the pernr in question on the below screen and execute the table.

 

Step 3: Double click the below entry to open the view list.

 

Step 4 : once the detail is opened, type /h on the top space where you enter the t-code

 

Step 5 : This opens a debugger; type CODE in variable 

 

Step 6 : Click on pencil icon next to SHOW and change the value to EDIT and execute the program

 

 

Step 7 : This open the screen for edit, please go ahead edit the screen and save the date.

 

 

Once completed with these steps, go ahead and integration the data. You will be able to see the data replicate successfully. 

 

Regards

Akash

entry.title: General Display Name Opt out in Second Half of 2024
entry.content:
General Display Name Opt out in Second Half of 2024

We are excited to announce the upcoming release for our opt out of General Display Name, so the employee name could be consistent globally in suite. Starting with the Second Half 2024 release, General Display Name will be enabled for all customers with the option to opt out. For the First Half 2025 release, General Display Name will become universal for all customers and the opt out option will be removed. 

 

General Display Name is release to customer two years ago. For now thousands of customers have enabled and been benefit from the feature. With General Display Name, customer can configure employee names that are expected to be displayed consistently across SAP SuccessFactors HXM Suite. A new admin option, Enable adoption of General Display Name, is added to control it. You can see this option under Admin Center -> Company System and Logo Settings. The new capability provides both consistency and flexibility to managing display names. With the new capability, general display names preferred by employees are displayed in all interactions where a legal name isn’t required, while administrators and HRBPs can also access a person’s legal name whenever needed. For details how to configure General Display Name please refer to https://community.sap.com/t5/human-capital-management-blogs-by-sap/adopting-general-display-name/ba-p/13536203
 
If customer has already enabled General Display Name, nothing will be changed. If customer haven't enable General Display Name but configured name format on People Profile, the name format will be used for General Display Name. For non-EC customers and for EC customers who haven't configure any name format, the system calculates the value according to message key COMMON_USER_DISPLAYNAME in company default locale, by default the message key is defined as a combination of first name, middle name and last name. If there is no full name, middle name, and last name, the field value is set to Unknown. To migrate to General Display Name, we need to run a job to generate display name according to name format configuration. That will be scheduled after new release downtime upgrade. Details please refer to below migration timetable
 

Migration timetable

Based on current release schedule, we will plan to run the General Display Name migration job as below: JobType: Migrate General Display Name

If the job is scheduled, you can see the scheduled job in Scheduled Job Manager. Track the status of the job in Scheduled Job Manager, you could have better idea when the upgrade is finished. 

Migration job will be run one week after downtime release window, for preview and production release window please refer to https://community.sap.com/t5/product-and-customer-updates/2024-product-release-dates-please-note-the-2h-2024-preview-release-date/ba-p/308079

Below is planned migration time for preview and production environment

Planned migration period for preview environment:

10/21/2024 12 : 00 AM to 10/28/2024 11:59 PM

Planned migration period for production environment:

11/24/2024 12 : 00 AM to 12/1/2024 11:59 PM

 

Have A Question Need Support?

If you encounter any issues while testing this feature, please create an incident with the component LOD-SF-PLT-MNG

 

Next Steps

• Once the Preview environment is upgraded with the 2H 2024 version, review the employee's name and consider if name format need to be configured, and prepare for the changes in Production
• Review the release note and the latest documentation in the Resources section below in order to understand the features supported
• Review and subscribe to the Customer Community blog to stay informed of the latest information on the change.
 

Resources

Customer Community Blog
https://community.sap.com/t5/human-capital-management-blogs-by-sap/adopting-general-display-name/ba-p/13536203
 
Help Portal
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_SUCCESSFACTORS_PLATFORM/70097a1a469d47a0ae08809e4a240f98/ac31628b0f804c2689585b3aca96eed9.html

Migration Progress Update

Migration progress will be updated in below table

DateFinished DC

entry.title: Skip workflow for Internal Hire from RCM to EC in Manage Pending Hire
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We have a requirement where there is a workflow triggered in Employee Central MSS when there is a change like Promotion/Demotion in Employee Central for an employee by managers. This workflow is written using a business rule on Job Information.

However, the same workflow is triggering when an internal candidate is hired as an Internal Hire in manage Pending Hire from Recruiting to EC directly on a job requisition. We don't want the workflow to be triggered in this scenario.

Is there any condition we can add to bifurcate the Manage Pending Hire scenario and EC MSS scenario. Please suggest if you have configured something similar basis your experience.

entry.title: Want to learn how to integrate SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central with SAP HCM for SAP S/4HANA?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Integrating SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central with SAP HCM for SAP S/4HANA learning journey.

Overview
The learning journey provides you with an overview of the main integration technology and implementation skills required when integrating SAP SuccessFactors with SAP S/4HANA.

Learning objectives
After completing this learning journey, consultants will be able to perform and configure the integration within SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central, SAP Cloud Integration middleware and SAP S/4HANA as well as access the resources and support available to support the integration.

Prerequisites

  • Getting Started with SAP HCM and HCM Payroll
  • Configuring SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Core

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area.

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Marriage or Paternity leave should not be taken twice for the same marriage date or same child DOB
entry.content:
Hi,

For this requirement, we have to create a custom marriage date or child date of birth (must be a mandatory field) in object 'Employee Time'. Now it will appear as below -

 

or 

now we need to write a take rule (Scenario: Absence Validation) as shown below -

or

Hope I am clear to you all. Thanks.

entry.title: Singapore CPF claw back & Excess Refund Guide for Employee Central Payroll Implementation
entry.content:
Introduction

It is a common customer requirement in the Singapore region to request for CPF refund for excess contributions made during the year in payroll. The statutory requirement to report on it and the need for the customer to internally report on it for claiming the refund through the CPF board is the major driver behind this requirement. The good news is that the Employee Central Payroll solution from SAP is capable of handling this legislative (and often, quite common) requirement from clients. 

Scenario Details

Its business as usual at ABC enterprises. Their payroll team has paid out a wage component to their employee in January. This wage component is considered for additional wages under CPF contribution. The payroll team performs the pay out and exits the payroll run for the month of January. A month passes by, the  payroll team realizes in the month of March, while finalizing the payroll data for a live run, that the payroll team paid the wage component in January, in error, to the employee and this needs to be nullified.

For this purpose, they need to perform a claw-back of the payment made in January to the employee, in the month of March. When they perform this claw-back, they also need to ensure the excess CPF paid & refund claim be performed in accordance to the amount that is due from CPF board, for both the employee and the employer portions.

Is this possible in Employee Central Payroll? A resounding, yes! 

Process Diagram

 

Detailed Understanding of Scenario Implementation in EC Payroll

1. Employee was paid a pay component in January '24 for SGD 5000

For the scenario we are discussing, the employee has been paid a wage type that is marked for CPF AW (additional wages) component. The input has flown in from employee central and employee central payroll made the payment for the wage type as below: 

2. In the month of April '24, the payroll team realizes that the wage component paid in January was, in fact, in error. They now need to claw back the payment to the employee and consequently, the CPF paid in error.

In order to perform this action, the customer has two options: 

A) They can either delete the pay component in the month of January '24 in Employee Central, perform the replication (i.e. let the replication do its job) and then run the live payroll for April month. This will help them to do two things at one shot with a clean design. Firstly, they will be able to perform the deletion without need to input any new wage component in the current month to nullify the payment in January. Hence, this reduces effort on the data entry team in EC as they no longer need to pull reports and check up on the correct value to input. Secondly, they also do not need several wage types configured as deductions with CPF AW marked for the claw back. 

In our scenario, the payroll team now goes ahead and runs the payroll for the employee after deleting the component from Employee Central. This generates a negative deduction and is also, automatically, a trigger for the CPF AW excess calculation. 

On the other hand, the customer can also choose to use a one time deduction pay component for the claw back however, this design is prone to having too many deduction components for the claw back in case there are several equivalent wage types that the client may be usually clawing back from the employee. 

Important point to note if a separate wage type is being used to claw back the CPF deducted, then it should be marked to CPF even if it is a deduction wage component. 

Also, it is common for some clients to claim the refund from CPF board in the month when the claw back happens. However, SAP will perform the recalculation only at the end of the year and hence the wage types to be used in wage type reporter to ascertain the amount would be /RC5 for the employee contributions and /RC7 for the employer contribution. 

3. YTD CPF recalculation in the EC Payroll system

In the month of December '24, the EC Payroll system does a recalculation of the CPF calculations for the employee and it is at this time that the CPF excess/shortfall is calculated. At this point, the client can generate the wage type reporter with the relevant wage types to check for any excess / shortfall for claims to CPF board. 

The wage type reporter can be used to generate the employee and employer CPF excess details as below: 

For the employee excess details, the wage types /311 & /31L are used as below: 

For the employer excess details, the wage types /313 and /31N are used as below: 

This same information is also available in IR8S section B as below, generated at year end. Needless to say, the recalculation happens at the time of termination (leaving the company) to provide the details on the excess/ shortfall of CPF. 

Once the refund has been claimed from the CPF board, the inputs in info type 3364 - Tax Declarations (SG)  is done by the employer to ensure completion of the entire cycle. This input is made into the sub-type 0002 of the info type 3364 - Tax Declarations (SG) as below: 

Once the input is maintained in IT 3364, the value automatically appears in IR8S under section C. This is basically the refund amount received from CPF. 

Conclusion

CPF claw back is an exception scenario in Singapore payroll that can be handled in the ways described above and reporting is possible either in the same month or at the year end when SAP recalculates the ceiling etc. Monthly reporting on the amount to be claimed back from CPF board is a bit unusual. Most clients use the YTD reports and the IR8S to check and claim the refunds.

entry.title: Setting Up the Latest Benefits Enrollment Experience: Step-by-Step Configuration and Helpful Tips
entry.content:
The latest Benefits Enrollment Experience within Employee Central Global Benefits has been designed to offer a guided enrollment process for employees. With the 1H 2024 release, this experience is available for three benefit types: Insurance, Pension, and Savings Plan.

Below are the key features of the Latest Benefits Enrollment Experience:

  • Admin can provide instructions and guidance to employees at every stage of the enrollment process.
  • The enrollment experience is fully localized and customizable.
  • Employees can compare different plans side by side and make effective decisions.
  • Progressive disclosure of benefits with dependencies.
  • Access concurrent Benefit Enrollment windows (Open Enrollment and Off Cycle).
  • Ability to manage dependents within the enrollment experience.
  • Support for evidence of insurability.
  • Available in the SAP SuccessFactors Mobile Application.

Please note that custom fields and on Change rules are not currently supported within the latest Benefits Enrollment Experience.

Let us now proceed with the end-to-end setup of the Latest Benefits Enrollment Experience. Prior to beginning the setup process, please ensure you have completed the following prerequisites:

Prerequisites:

  • Go to Scheduled Job Manager and create/run Sync Employee Central Dependents with Benefits Job for all required legal entities. This is a one-time Job to sync existing EC Dependent data within Benefits. All new changes will be synced automatically once employee saves the dependent information.

  • Use newly delivered Config UIs for both Benefit (BenefitConfigUI_Version2) and Insurance Plan (BenefitInsurancePlan_Version2). These config UIs contains a new set of fields that are required to set up the latest Benefits Enrollment Experience.
  • Provide permissions to access the newly delivered objects (Please refer Latest Benefits Enrollment Experience -> Role-Based Permissions for the Latest Benefits Enrollment Experience section in the Employee Central Global Benefits help documentation for details)
     

Step 1: Adding Benefit Actions block to People Profile

The first step involves adding the Benefit Actions block to the People Profile to access the new experience. Here's how to do it:

  • Navigate to Configure People Profile
  • Search for Benefit Actions block
  • Place the block in the Benefit section

Step 2: Building the Benefit Enrollment Process

To build the Benefit Enrollment Process, follow these steps:

  • Go to Benefit Admin Overview
  • Create Configure Benefit Enrollment Process
  • Fill in details like Effective From, Configuration ID, Configuration Name, Enrollment Type, Open Enrollment Schedule, Currency etc.
  • Create Configure Benefit Enrollment Process Template for both Regular Open Enrollment and Off Cycle Enrollment.
  • Assign the Benefit Enrollment Process templates created in the above step to the Benefit Enrollment Process.
  • Assign the Legal Entities.
  • Select the Benefits you would like to include in the Benefit Enrollment Process.

Quick Tips:

  • The enrollment start and end date should be same for all Benefits.
  • Use BB codes to format Introduction Description and Family Member Description text if required. Supported BB codes 
  • Add tokens like [[benefitYear]] to dynamically retrieve the data. (For a complete list of tokens, refer to the "Assigning Benefit Enrollment Process Templates" section in the Employee Central Global Benefits help documentation).
  • Assign templates for both Open Enrollment and Off-cycle enrollment.
  • Setup customized Terms and Conditions for both Regular Open Enrollment and Off Cycle Enrollment (You can use BB codes to format the Terms and Conditions text)
  • Once you have built and saved the Benefit Enrollment Process, navigate to Benefit Actions block in the People Profile and validate the link (make sure the enrollment window is open)

You can refer below screenshots to build the Benefit Enrollment Process

Benefit Enrollment Process

Benefit Enrollment Process Template

Step 3: Updating details in the Benefit Configuration

Each benefit type within the latest Benefits Enrollment Experience includes specific fields and settings. These fields are only available in the newly delivered config UI for Benefit (BenefitConfigUI_Version2). Please select BenefitConfigUI_Version2 to access all fields in the Benefits Admin Overview.

Please be aware that these fields are in addition to the ones configured for Benefit setup in the legacy experience. If you need details about Benefit Configuration in the legacy experience, please refer to the Employee Central Global Benefits help documentation. The below fields are only required for the Latest Benefits Enrollment Experience.

Quick Tips:

  • If you are unable to access any of these fields, please confirm if visibility of the field is set as Editable in the Object Definition. 
  • If you encounter any issues accessing Standard Fields, Additional Fields, or Allowed Dependent Types when updating Benefit details, navigate to the Configure Object Definition and verify that the visibility of the insuranceFieldConfiguration, insPlanCardConfiguration, and allowedDependents associations within the Insurance Benefit Details object is set to editable.

Insurance

  • Enrollment Applicable For: Specify whether the Benefit is applicable for Employee and Dependents or for Dependent Only.
  • Beneficiary Type: Specify the applicable Beneficiary Type and assign the Beneficiary Field Configuration to specify additional fields for both Family Member and Other type beneficiaries.
  • Allowed Dependent Types: Specify which dependent types are allowed for this Benefit. If it's just applicable for Self/Employee, do not maintain any value. Note: If Dependent Relationship Type dropdown is not visible under Allowed Dependent Type section, please update the visibility of the field in the Object Definition.
  • Insurance Plan Standard Fields: Specify the standard Insurance Plan fields that should be displayed on the Plan cards.
  • Insurance Plan Additional Fields: Specify the additional information that should be displayed on the Plan cards for comparison between plans. Additional fields can be configured to provide extra details about the plans, such as Coinsurance, Emergency Room Services, and others for Health Plans.
  • Tobacco Check: Indicate whether the Tobacco Check is relevant for this Benefit. If applicable, maintain the rate charts with values as Yes/No, or No Selection if not applicable.
  • Health Check: Indicate whether the Health Check is relevant for this Benefit. If applicable, maintain the rate charts with values as Yes/No, or No Selection if not applicable.

Quick Tips:

  • Ensure to maintain comprehensive Benefit and Plan Descriptions to effectively communicate the necessary information to users.
  • Accurately maintain Allowed Dependent Types as it determines the visibility of dependents on the enrollment UI.
  • Ensure that Tobacco and Health Check in the Rate Chart is accurately maintained, as they directly impact the rates displayed on the enrollment UI.

Pension

  • Additional Help Text: Maintain the additional help text under 'Define the Additional Help Text to Display on the Enrollment Screen' section. This help text will be displayed in the right panel of the Pension enrollment UI.

Note: If the contribution type selected is Percentage, ensure to maintain the Pay Component for employee and employer contributions to calculate the estimated contribution.

Savings Plan

  • Additional Help Text: Maintain the additional help text under 'Define the Additional Help Text to Display on the Enrollment Screen' section. This help text will be displayed in the right panel of the Savings Plan enrollment UI.

Step 4: Set Payment Frequency 

Maintain the Payment Frequency in the assigned Pay Group for the user. Go to Manage Data -> Pay Group -> Update Payment Frequency. Payment Frequency will be displayed next to the total cost on the enrollment UI.

Step 5: Maintain Help and Resources

In the latest Benefits Enrollment Experience, you have the option to provide helpful resources for each Benefit. When you add any of the helpful resources such as Policy Documents, Forms, Hyperlinks, or Benefit Contacts in the Benefit configuration, they will be displayed under Help and Resources on the enrollment UI.

Step 6: Enable Beneficiaries Setting

If beneficiaries are applicable for any Benefit (Insurance, Pension or Savings Plan), you can activate the Beneficiaries setting in the Benefit Configuration by choosing the Beneficiary Type as either Primary or Primary or Secondary. By default, the Beneficiary Name, Relationship, and Percentage fields will be enabled in the enrollment UI, with the option to include additional fields to collect more details for the beneficiaries (Additional fields such as Date of Birth, Email, Phone Number, Address, and Identification Number can be configured using Beneficiaries Field Configuration).

The Beneficiaries Field Configuration field will only appear when you select the Beneficiary Type as either Primary or Secondary.

Note: Ensure that permissions for the Beneficiaries Field Configuration object, or any other relevant object, are enabled if you encounter issues accessing it in the Benefits Admin Overview.

Step 7: Configuring Terms and Conditions

In the updated Benefits Enrollment Experience, you have the option to include Terms and Conditions text for each template (Open Enrollment or Off-cycle). You can add the T&C text in the Benefit Enrollment Process template.

To do this, go to Benefits Admin Overview -> Configure Benefit Enrollment Process Template -> Enable Terms and Conditions -> Add the Terms and Conditions text.

I hope this blog is helpful. You may reach out to me for any information.

Thank you for reading.

Eshali (Product Management)

entry.title: Business Rule for Retro ‘Hire Date Correction’ In SuccessFactors EC to avoid Payroll impacts
entry.content:
Business Scenario:

Payroll is typically affected by business events such as pay grade changes, cost-center adjustments, and retroactive changes in pay. Furthermore, we will address a particular business case that involves correcting the hire date backwards and determining how to limit this modification to prevent any negative effects on payroll.   

Currently, using Hire date correction tool you are allowed to update the Hire date to a backdate. For e.g.: A user is active in the system for 2 years, you can make changes to the hire date which impacts the payroll process. A straightforward business rule can be implemented to limit changes to the hire date with a back date/Retro date.

Configuration Steps:

Step-1: The below rule explains a basic logic to restrict with prior date hire date to the current hire date change.

  • Create Message Definition under Admin tool:  Manage Data -> Message Definition -> Click on Create New -> Select ‘Message Definition’, Then ‘Save’.

 

  • In this article as an example:  External code: HIREDATE_CORRECTION_RESTRICT

 

 

Step-2:  Create Business rule and assign the Message definition in the rule:

  • Create a business rule in the ‘Configure Business rule’ with a base object: Hire Date Correction and Scenario:  Rules for MDF Based Objects:
  • Assign Message Definition:  HIREDATE_CORRECTION_RESTRICT in the Business rule.

 

 

Step-3: Go to Admin Tool:  Configure Object Definition -> Object Definition -> Object: Hire Date Correction:

 

  • Click ‘Take Action ‘-> Make Correction:

  • Click on the Field: ‘newHireDate’ -> Details

 

  •  Assign the business rule created under Rules section in the field: newHireDate -> Details:

 

Step-4:  Go to Manage Data:

  • Search ‘Hire Date Correction’ -> Create New: Hire Date Correction

  • In the below example user: Jessica Walker: 
  • Enter a Correction Name
  • Enter Employee ID or Name
  • Review the Current Hire Date

Example: Updated the New Hire date 12/31/2018 which is before the Current Hire date: 01/01/2019 -> Save.   The above assigned error will be displayed.

 

 

 

 

Additional Notes:

  • This rule will work even in imports, only validate that the user executing the imports has assigned the permission to trigger rules on import. More details Configuring Business Rules for Data Imports

Error message after import:

entry.title: Deduction of Leaves during Public Holidays
entry.content:
SAP had introduced the Absence Counting configuration somewhere on the same lines to take care of complexities of public holiday deductions.

Requirement -We had a certain section of employees who work on the shop floor and do not have a fixed holiday schedule as such. This section of employee work on public holidays and then claim the holiday back as compensatory off.

Create a holiday work Pattern:

  1. Enable the standard field -holiday-work-pattern-code in Job-Information Portlet

  1. Create a Holiday Work Pattern

Manage data -->Holiday Work Pattern

Once you have created this holiday work pattern, this holiday work pattern needs to be assigned to all such employees who are supposed to be working on a Public Holiday.

Note- As the holiday calendar is on a year to year  basis, ensure to assign this work pattern from the beginning of the holiday calendar. For example -If your holiday calendar has a start date of 01/01/2024 for the current year, Assign this Holiday pattern with a split from 01/01/2024 in the employees Job Info Portlet.

Holiday Planned Working Time Determination

There is a SAP standard provided object,Holiday Planned Working Time Determination, this object determines the Planned working time of the employees based on a combination of holiday Category which are assigned to the public Holidays and the holiday work Pattern.

Holiday Planned Working Time Determination already exists in the system and if you have the requisite permissions, you will be able to view this object in manage data.

“No Planned Working Time” is the default entry available here and will be used as default if no other entry is present.

You need to define your pattern here to determine the planned working time as per your requirement.

For example -Our requirement was that, if the employees are working on a holiday, system should treat it as a normal working day and deduct the  leave if employee applies for a TimeOff.

Assign the previously created Holiday work pattern in step 1 - here.

Details-

So according to the requirement, if it is a full day holiday, then these employees should be working as per their scheduled planned working time.

Time information without work holiday Pattern assigned in Job Info.The planned working time for Holidays will show as Zero.

After assigning the Holiday Pattern in Job Info Portlet, the holidays now have the regular planned working time displayed.

Now if an employee applies for Compensatory off during this public holiday period, The leaves will be deducted accordingly.

Important:

Since this approach changes the planned working time of the employee, you need to adjust the other time types, where the deduction is based on work schedule. This is where the Absence counting configuration comes into picture.

Now suppose for the same period if the employee applies annual leave, then the system should not deduct the public holidays. Time types which are based on calendar days, do not require any change.

Absence Counting Configuration

Manage data-->Time Type-->Annual Leave

The combination of holiday Selection, planned working time selection and weekdays selection is especially important.

For our requirement we want the rule to be executed only during the public holiday, So select- Include holidays and other sections are left blank.

Create a Rule to not deduct the Public holidays  and assign it to the Absence counting rule in the Absence counting configuration.

The above rule will set the deduction quantity to Zero whenever there is a public holiday for annual leave.

So, if an employee applies for an annual leave, his deduction will be zero on a public holiday due to the Absence Counting Configuration, whereas if he applies for a Compensatory off then his public holidays will be deducted due to the Holiday planned working time determination.

Example- 1 day Public Holiday(15thSep) between the leave period 14thSep-16thSep

Annual leave deduction-No Public Holiday deduction-Shows 2 days deduction.

Compensatory off -Includes Public Holiday deduction-Shows 3 days deduction

Similiarly for other  leave types also , you can define if you want the Public holiday to be deducted or not deducted.

entry.title: Safe (and happy) Cycling!
entry.content:
We’ve all become experts at constant movement, whether we’re joining new groups, coaching our friends, or planning our next big adventure.

SAP innovations are delivered in cycles that are familiar to you, putting solutions in your hands that simplify everyday work, increase your effectiveness, and secure your success. Because we know that your finance and HR teams collaborate as they plan and reconcile budgets with employee position and financing requirements, we are releasing continuous improvements to our platform that give you easier, real-time controls. And yes, we've built in AI-powered insights, recommendations, and automation. 

SAP’s public sector solution uses SAP SuccessFactors HCM alongside SAP S/4HANA Cloud and SAP Business AI, for end-to-end management of strategic, financial, HR and operational decisions for budgeting and forecasting. In the upcoming release cycles (2H2024 and 1H2025) you’ll be able to manage position budget control with all the compliance and automation that you’ve come to expect from your industry-minded neighbors here at SAP. See how people are managed, and if positions are funded, alongside grant reservation and commitments. Look forward to conversations with colleagues that shape policy and efficiently run programs, and make informed, fast financial and operational decisions with live access to intuitive dashboards and simulation tools. Speed along your chosen path to people assignments, finish payroll and accounting runs, and complete the financial reporting of budgets and grants; proactively monitor employee costs, use automated anomaly detection and prevention to mitigate fraud risks and reduce losses. Adopt these features at your own pace.

Subscribe to keep moving ahead: SAP SuccessFactors What's New and SAP S/4HANA Cloud 

It’s a comprehensive and unified approach that increases any organizations’ stewardship of taxpayer money. No matter if your agency has 500 people, 5,000 people, or is larger: we help governments and not-for-profits make data driven decisions that carry their mission forward.

entry.title: Long service award payment to employees as One Time Payment
entry.content:
We got a requirement wherein based on the length of service of employee, a non-recurring pay component should be populated with certain amount automatically on employees profile under Non- Recurring portlet. The amount differs based on the legal entities. Below is the table of the same. So, if an employee completes 1826 days in the legal entity 3 then $500 should be given to that employee, if employee completes 3652 days in the legal entity 1 then $150 should be given and so on.

 

This requirement is not possible via business rules. To meet this requirement, we have created different integration jobs.

We have followed below steps:

  • First, we have configured a new field “Length of service” with data type as “Double” under Employment information.

Please note we must use data type as “Double” else we won’t be able to put the calculation on this field in the subsequent integration jobs.

Please see the below screenshot of the same.

 

 

  • Next, we have created an integration job which will capture length of service based on employee “Original Start date” and updates the Length of service field under Employment information.

This integration job is created with base as “EmpEmployment”. On Length of service field, we have put a calculation which calculates the length in days based on Original start date. 

 

Note: We have put calculation in days because with months we are not getting the round figure. Hence, we used “Days”.

 

 

  • We have created another field which is “Length of your service” as “custom-string3”.

This field is required in the later integrations jobs to put a filter to run the integration jobs.

 According to SAP, apart from standard fields only custom fields with string1,2 and 3 will be available for filters in the integration job. No other custom fields will be available for filer under the integration job from Employment info section

So, we have created this additional field. Below is the screenshot of the field configuration.

 

 

 

  • We have created another integration job which will copy the data from Length of service field (Field which we have created in Step 1) to Length of your service field (Field which we have created in Step 3).

This integration base is also EmpEmployement Info. Below is the screenshot of the calculation which we have put for Length of your service field.

 

 

 

Both the above jobs must run in sequence. The job in Step 2 must run first and then the job which we have created in Step 4.

 

Now, we got the length of service in days based on the above integration jobs.

 

  • Next, we created the last 3 integration jobs for each legal entity as the amounts are different for each legal entity on completion of number of days in company.

Starting entity for this job is “EmpEmployment”. Associate this to OneTimePayments where the pay component should be populated on the employee profile.

  • Under Configure fields, maintain the default value of the pay component. In our case its 7017 (pay component code).Below is the screenshot of the same.

 

              

 

       

  • Issue date field is configured as calculated field, and the calculation will set the issue date as today. i.e. on the day the integration job runs.
  • Amount field is also configured as a calculated field. We have put If/Then statement on this field.

The statement says if say Length of service field value is 3652 then amount value should be 150 for legal entity 1and so on. Below is the screenshot of the calculation:

                             

 

  • Next, we move on to Filter and sort, here we have put filters on legal entity and on number of days. We have put filter on number of days because if we don’t put filter then for employees whose length of service is other than the number of days mentioned above, then amount 0 is populating under One Time Payment section for employees and we don’t want the same. Hence, we put filters. Below is the screenshot of the same:

 

 

 

        Similarly, we have created the integration job for other legal entities and schedule the job at last. So, once the first two jobs ran only then this job must run to get the correct length of service.

Thank you for reading the blog. I hope this will help you in meeting certain requirements. Your feedback, questions, and suggestions are always welcome.  We’d love to hear about your experiences, custom requirements, or any insights you have, so please feel free to share them in the comments section below. Happy Learning 

SAP Integration Suite SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central

entry.title: Absence Leave Type Configuration in SuccessFactors Employee Central
entry.content:
Dear viewers,

You have found a blog with a real time scenario achieved in the SF EC. It is configured in the sales demo SF system.

Aim : To allow employee to take grandparent leave based on eligibility.

Conditions:

1.Employee should enter grandchild date of birth while applying the leave, he can avail this leave within 30 days from the date of the grandchild born.

In a single request, he can avail 3 days, not more than 3 days allowed to apply. And in his employment period he can avail only 3 times.

Configuration steps in the below.

You can maintain grandchild record either in the dependents portlet and can be achieved using different rule. But in this case, I have configured to maintain grandchild data while applying for the leave and followed by workflow approval with the attachment proof.

1. In employee time object, I have added two fields to enter relationship and date of birth.


2. Since this field has to be visible only while selecting the grand parent leave, I have given field criteria for this time type alone. Below screenshot is for your reference. You can see this section in bottom of the field details.

Configure the rule in the time type take rules to validate.

 Below is the output tested cases.

This output shows attachment mandatory. You can define in the take rules.

Scenario 3: Employee when try to apply 3 days, within 30 days from the grand child born date, system allowed. The below screenshot is for your reference.

Scenario 4: If employee try to apply before the grandchild born date. System will throw error.

Scenario 5 : If employee try to apply after 30 from the grand child born date. System will throw error.

When applying for the fourth time, system throws a error.


Thanks for referring to my blog. Will reach you in next blog with a more interesting scenario.

Regards,
Aravind R
HXM consultant.

entry.title: Setting Up the Crossboarding Process with SAP SuccessFactors Recruitment Management
entry.content:
The Internal Hire process facilitates a smooth transition for current employees into new roles within our organization.

Onboarding for an internal hire begins with the onboarding initiation and concludes when the employee steps into their new position. The process can be initiated through:

  • SAP SuccessFactors Recruiting
  • SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central
  • An external Applicant Tracking System (ATS)

Importantly, the employee's User ID and Username will remain unchanged during the internal hire.

  • Enable Enhanced Internal Hire Process

The enhanced onboarding process for internal hires leverages the functionalities of Centralized Services in Employee Central to improve the backend data validation processes in Onboarding.

Navigate to Onboarding General Settings>Turn on Enable Enhanced Internal Hire Process

 

  • Enable Internal Hire Options in Manage Data:

Navigate to Manage Data>Select Onboarding Configuration> Select Yes for Centralized Services in Employee Central for Enhanced Internal Hire Process Enabled

Navigate to Manage Data>Select Onboarding Configuration> Select Yes for enhancedCrossboardingOnSL

 

  • Create a Process Variant:

Navigate to Process Variant Manager>click + Process Variant>Add Process Variant dialog box opens>Select the process variant type from the Process Type dropdown, and provide a unique ID and, a Name for the process flow. and click save.

  • Crossboarding Program

We can define programs to manage crossboarding-related tasks, roles, and responsibilities. We can set up specific and relevant tasks for the organization and determine responsible user groups for the tasks. To perform this activity.

Navigate to Manage Onboarding and Offboarding Tasks>Click on + New Program>Add details and Task list from + Add Task option and save.

 

  • Setting Up a Rule for Selecting the Onboarding Program

Define business rules to select the program based on conditions.

Navigate to Configure Business Rules>Click on Add Rule>Select Select Onboarding Task Configuration from the Onboarding tab>Enter the Rule Name, Rule ID, and select a Start Date>Define the rule as per business requirements>Save

 

  • Set up the Future Manager Job Relationship

To ensure the future manager can complete key onboarding tasks before the internal hire’s start date, it’s important to define their role. This picklist value acts as a clear identifier, making it easy to assign the right permissions and responsibilities to those in the future manager position.

Add a picklist value:

Picklist Center>Search Picklist jobRelType>Click on +(Add)>Enter managers (internal hire) as the External Code, provide the Label as Future Manager, and set the Status to Active.

 

  • Permission Role for Future Manager

Manage Permission Roles>Create New Role>Update details like Name, descriptions, and permissions> Under Grant this role to dropdown, choose Future Manager (Internal Hire) and save.

  • Add Event Reasons

Configure the event reason so it can be applied in the business rule to trigger the internal hire process.

Navigate to Manage Organization, Pay and Job Structures>Create New>Event Reason>Enter the details and save.

 

  • Configure Onboarding Initiation Business Rule

  • Configure a Transfer Event Reason

  • Assign Rule to Job Information

Go to Manage Business Configuration>under HRIS Elements select jobInfo>Scroll down to the trigger rules section>set the Transfer rule for the Event Type as onPostSave.

 

  • Configure Event Reason Picklist

After completing the crossboarding setup, The next step in populating event reasons in recruitment is to add all transfer-related event reasons to a picklist, ensuring they have the same external code as the event reason code in Manage Organization, Pay, and Job Structures.

 

  • Configure Event Reason Field in Recruitment

The next step is to add a picklist field in recruitment, either in the job application or job requisition. Then, assign the necessary permissions, such as allowing R to edit this field during the onboarding or offer stages.

 

  • Recruit-to-Hire Data Mapping

The next step is to map the event reason field from Recruitment to Onboarding, allowing the system to automatically pre-populate the event reason value when Onboarding is initiated.

  •  Business Rule to Populate Job Information Based on Position

There are two ways to update the internal hire's job information when initiating crossboarding from recruitment:

  1. Map all job information fields directly from recruitment.
  2. Create a business rule to populate job information using the position details.

After creating the rule, assign it to the position field under job information in Manage Business Configuration.

Note: Please make sure only the context of the “Onboarding” rule is true.

In all scenarios, we need to have the same rule twice. One with all other contexts as true and another one with the “Onboarding” rule context as true.

  • Initiate Crossboarding Using Recruitment

Once the job is posted internally and the Internal Hire has completed all the necessary steps in recruitment. The recruiter needs to select the correct event reason for recruitment and initiate crossboarding using the Initiate Onboarding button under the action menu.

 

  • Complete Review Employee Information Step

Once crossboarding is successfully initiated by recruitment, the candidate will be visible on the home page Onboarding tile or in the Onboarding dashboard.

Click on the candidate’s name and complete the Review Employee Information Step
Under the job information section, you can view the new job information of the internal hire.
You can also click on the “Clock” icon just to see the changes in job information.

You can validate the source as Crossboarding.

Currently, Crossboarding is supported for the below entities only:

JOB_RELATIONS_INFO
PAY_COMPONENT_NON_RECURRING
PAY_COMPONENT_RECURRING
EMP INFO
JOB INFO
COMP INFO

Note: In the above scenario we are only passing the job information.

Conclusion:

The Internal Hire process provides an excellent chance for companies to utilize the skills and expertise of their current employees.

SAP SuccessFactors is a good tool that supports internal hire and crossboarding processes within companies. Its customizable features enable organizations to adapt the internal hire and crossboarding processes to meet their unique needs.

Useful Link:

https://me.sap.com/notes/2970083

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_SUCCESSFACTORS_ONBOARDING/c94ed5fcb5fe4e0281f396556743812c/da569f9782e8458daf687d18e52ea4ab.html

entry.title: Internal Hiring: Recruiting-to-Employee Central
entry.content:
Requirement

Implement Crossboarding alongside Onboarding 2.0 in SAP SuccessFactors to facilitate internal transitions and employee movements.

Limitations of Crossboarding

While implementing the crossboarding solution for one of the businesses, we encountered several issues that could not be resolved using the crossboarding module. Some of the key challenges include:

  • The HR team cannot review crossboarding letters before they are sent to the candidate or internal employee.
  • When a candidate is moved to the Onboarding bucket and onboarding is initiated, data is automatically published to Employee Central. This results in incomplete or inaccurate records in EC(even before crossboarding is completed), disrupting the smooth transition of employees.
  • Compensation and Job Information data are being overwritten if not transmitted from the Recruiting system, leading to confusion and potential data loss.
  • Crossboarding 2.0 allows only a limited set of pay components to flow from the Recruitment Management module to Employee Central. This creates a challenge in capturing the full compensation details accurately, especially when additional pay components are needed.
Solution – Internal Hiring via Recruitment Management

The challenge with crossboarding arises as soon as onboarding is triggered. To resolve this, we can bypass the onboarding trigger and exclude internal hires from the crossboarding process. Instead of utilizing crossboarding, internal employees can be directly hired from Recruiting into Employee Central. This can be achieved by introducing a custom filter field that distinguishes between job requisitions for internal and external hires. This field will determine whether the hiring process follows the Recruiting-to-Employee Central route or the Recruiting-to-Onboarding-to-Employee Central route.

Implementation Steps

In order to implement the internal hiring process using Recruiting-to-Employee Central, below are the steps to be implemented. The diagram below shows a quick overview of the process.

 

1. Add a Filter Field on the Job Requisition Details Page.

Navigate to Manage Templates -> Recruiting Management -> Job Requisition -> Select the relevant Job Requisition Template -> Add the Standard Filter Field (e.g., filter1). For example, the field could be labeled "Type of Hire" (Internal Hiring / External Hiring).

The "Type of Hire" field will be a picklist.

Ensure the appropriate view and edit permissions are granted to stakeholders based on business requirements. In this example, HR and Recruiters are given edit access, while all other stakeholders have view-only permissions.

You can configure additional filters in the Onboarding Integration Setup page by defining filter fields in the job requisition template. There are up to 15 custom filter fields (filter1-15) that can be created in the job requisition template.

Sample Field Definition of Filter Fields:

<field-definition id="filter1" type="picklist" required="false" custom="true">
<field-label><![CDATA[Type of Hire]]></field-label>
<picklist-id>test_ONBreq_RCM</picklist-id>
</field-definition>

 

 

 

 

2. Add the filter field under custom filters Internal and External Career Search Settings.

Go to Internal and External Career Search Settings and locate the custom filters section. For the filter field created (filter1), update the field label and picklist ID accordingly. Ensure this update is applied under both the External and Internal Site Tabs.

 

3. Update the field Criteria for Onboarding Integration

In the Settings tab, you can enable Onboarding either for all job requisitions or for specific ones. Use the available dropdown fields to define the criteria for job requisitions where Onboarding will be applied.

Navigate to Onboarding Integration Setup and select the radio button labeled "Apply Onboarding to Job Requisitions that meet ANY of the following criteria."

The first dropdown field will display the available filters for the requisition, and the second dropdown will show the corresponding values for those filters. Select "filter1" and choose the appropriate value to trigger the onboarding process.

 

 

 

 

This means that Onboarding will only be triggered if the "Type of Hire" is set to "External Recruit." If the "Type of Hire" is set to "Internal Recruit," the Initiate Onboarding button will not be available. Once the candidate is moved to the Onboarding status, they will directly appear under Manage Pending Hire – Recruitment.

Please ensure that the filter value is correctly updated in the job requisitions for the system to validate and apply the filter and integration settings.

 

 

 

4. Create Internal Hiring templates.

Offer letter and offer approval templates in Recruiting can be tailored specifically for the internal hiring process. These templates function similarly to those for external hires, following the same process for sending letters and obtaining offer approvals.

Incorporating these additional steps into your external hiring process will enable the internal hiring process to follow the same structure, ensuring consistency while preventing data loss and issues for internal employees.

Solution Benefits

  • The direct Recruiting Management to Employee Central solution ensures that existing employee data, such as pay components and other compensation details in EC, are preserved, preventing the loss of critical information during the internal transfer process. During Manage Pending Hire, HR has the opportunity to validate everything before updates are made to EC.
  • Only the data updated during the recruitment process is reflected in Manage Pending Hire; all other information remains unchanged in EC.
  • HR teams can review offer letters and new contracts before they are sent to the candidate, ensuring accuracy and compliance.
  • Data is transferred to EC only after the internal hiring process is finalized in the Manage Pending Hires tool. This prevents premature publication of information in EC, ensuring that all data is complete and accurate before the employee's new role is updated in the system.

Please note, that if workflows in Employee Central are triggered by data changes, they will be activated when changes are submitted in Manage Pending Hire. These workflows should be adjusted to avoid triggering during the internal hire process if not required by the business. Additionally, the Event and Event Reason fields must be manually filled in Manage Pending Hire.

Conclusion

Implementing a direct Recruiting Management to Employee Central process for internal hires offers a reliable and efficient solution, bypassing the complexities of Crossboarding. By tailoring the hiring process with custom filters, internal hiring templates, and thorough validation in Manage Pending Hires, businesses can ensure accurate data transfer and minimize errors. This approach preserves critical employee information, enhances HR oversight, and simplifies the overall internal mobility process. With the right configurations, companies can reduce administrative overhead, improve data integrity, and provide a positive experience for both HR and employees during internal transitions.

By adopting this solution, organizations can optimize their internal hiring workflows, fostering smoother transitions and better management of employee data within SAP SuccessFactors.

This solution was accepted and was implemented for the business. Hope this helps you to define an alternative in case you are facing similar issues.

Please feel free to drop a comment for any questions or queries or share your way of handling these problems. Thank you for reading this blog. Happy Learning 🙂

entry.title: Transforming Your HR Operating Model with SAP SuccessFactors Implementation
entry.content:
In today's fast-paced business environment, staying ahead requires not just innovation but a keen understanding of operational processes, technology, and adaptable operating models.

On Thursday 12th September 2024, Dr Andreas Eckert, Global HR Advisor, and Klaus Kampen, SAP’s Business Architect for HR Innovation and Transformation, delved into the potential impact and opportunities for your HR operating model through the implementation of SAP SuccessFactors. 

For us at SAP, success is when customers actually realize the value that they expect from their investment in our solutions. But obviously value doesn't realize itself. A solution itself doesn't provide you with the value. It's important to design the processes that actually drive the system configuration, with some principles in mind on how they best perform the best in your specific context. 

Important processes though, are not only just things that happen along a chain of a chain of events. Processes also include the actors that are actively contributing and doing tasks along these processes. So the operating model of HR informs and must inform the process design. And this is where the concept of SAP’s leading practices comes in.

SAP SuccessFactors has a number of leading practices across all solution areas that actually encapsulate and condensate consulting experience and combine that with product development and design principles to inform process design to inform the operating model duties and help move forward. 

The webinar focussed on the 1st building block out of the critical steps chain that need to be taken to arrive to the point of value: 

Key Insights from the Webinar:

  • Learning from Top Performers: Using a sports example, we explored how leading organizations use SAP SuccessFactors to streamline HR processes and drive business success.
  • Impact of Leading Practices: SAP SuccessFactors’ best practices provide a strong foundation for transforming HR functions across consultancy, expertise, and operations. (Independently from your particular HR Operating Model) There are 5 essential functions that exist in all HR Operating Modell that assume responsibilities and that will be impacted by the implementation of SAP SuccessFactors
  • Real-Life Customer Example: We demonstrated how a customer successfully applied SAP SuccessFactors leading practice recommendations to overcome manual processes, scale HR operations, and align with their growth strategy.
  • Workshop Insights: Through our Impact and Opportunity workshops, we highlighted the importance of mapping roles and responsibilities to ensure clarity and efficiency in process design. 
  • Process Design and Configuration: We emphasized how defined tasks and roles translate into detailed process diagrams, which guide the technical configuration of SAP SuccessFactors.
  • HR Leading Practice Advisory Service: We introduced our advisory service, which supports organizations in aligning their HR models with SAP SuccessFactors best practices for a smooth and tailored implementation.

The implementation of SAP SuccessFactors can elevate your HR operations by aligning HR with business goals, automating key processes, enhancing employee experience, and enabling data-driven decision-making. Organizations that are prepared to embrace this change will unlock new opportunities for growth, innovation, and efficiency.

HR Leading Practices Advisory Service is part of the SAP SuccessFactors Services portfolio that aims to support customers interested in applying leading practices to elevate their own Operating Model and achieve value expected from the SAP SuccessFactors features and function by: 

  • Defining the right HR concepts and select the right scope 
  • Evaluating the potential change impact on your HR organization
  • Prepare a smooth SAP SuccessFactors implementation with a well- equipped project team that has made key design decisions in advance 

If you are interested in learning more, please don’t hesitate to reach out to: SAPHIT@SAP.com

Resources: 

Webinar Recording

Slides

entry.title: Original Start Date, Hire Date, Seniority Date. What’s the difference?
entry.content:
Original Start Date, Hire Date, Seniority Date. What’s the difference?

 

“Original Start Date”

The “Original Start Date” field is used to store the very 1st Hire date for an Employee when hiring an employee via the “Add New Employee”, the “Manage Pending Hires” or the “Manage Inactive Employee” wizards.

 

It gets populated with the 1st Hire record Effective Start Date that an employee account receives when he/she is Hired into the system.

“Hire date”

This initial hire date comes from the Hire Date field but…

 

If the employee is Terminated and Rehired, their hire date field under “Employment Details” section will be updated to display the employee's latest hire record.  

“Seniority start date”

This date field can change as per the company's policy.

 

E.g.: There may be a policy that the seniority date will start once the Employee completes probation of 6 months. In this case the hire date will remain as the employee's hire date, however “Seniority Date” can be updated to the hire date + 6 months.

 

Original Hire date: What happens if I “rehire with old employment.”?

The Original Start Date field usually gets populated by the Effective Start Date of the Job Info "Hire" record (with the Hire event) - the very first time the employee is hired.  

The system will automatically correct the “Original Start Date” to when the first Job Info Hire record is re-imported.

 

Original Hire date: What happens if I “rehire with new employment.”?

When you rehire an inactive employee with the new employment wizard, the Original Start Date will not be same as old employment's Original Start Date, because the new employment will have new user id and Original start date for new employment will have the Date as First Jobinfo record with Hire event.

 

Seniority Start Date, what’s SuccessFactors standard behavior?

For new hires, the seniority start date field will have the same date as the hire date.

For rehires, the seniority start date will default from the rehire date due to the following:

When rehiring the user, the system disregards the seniority that was built up in the previous employment and starts from scratch as of the rehire date.

 

Condensed links

2080126 - How do Employment Information > Hire Date and Original Start Date fields work? - Employee Central

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/2080126

2948707 - EC: Difference between SeniorityDate and HireDate in Employment Details

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/2948707

2487332 - Why is Seniority Start Date Defaulted From Rehire Date

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/2487332

Changing an Employee's Hire Date in Employee Central

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_SUCCESSFACTORS_EMPLOYEE_CENTRAL/4b0f4a72b0ea46fb9b9917bdd7c8f29b/722047f75ba54c9198b273c031a93422.html
